newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The June 9 AGM (dividend raised to EUR2.80, +7.7%) was as-scripted and previously announced; Q1 order intake of EUR1,512m (+5.3% y/y) and confirmed FY26 targets (3-5% organic revenue growth, EBITDA margin 10.7-11.1%) predate the window.

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent AI-node lens: packaging-automation tie to precision assembly cells is thesis-stage and untouched by the as-scripted June 9 AGM. No new datapoint links Krones to the AI supply chain. Hold rank 66.
verdicts.bull: Strong Q1 orders (+5.3%) and confirmed FY26 targets show a resilient, well-run automation franchise on a de-rated multiple (YTD -16%).
verdicts.neutral: Solid beverage-packaging cyclical; AI-node relevance remains a speculative adjacency with no concrete evidence.
verdicts.bear: Core demand is food/beverage capex, not AI; the latent AI-node case is a stretch with no design wins or DC-adjacent contracts.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any explicit move into precision assembly/inspection for electronics or DC hardware.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Beverage capex cyclicality; thesis never materializes into AI relevance.
